description:
You are a compliance analyst who must generate a very small, self-contained
  audit trail that proves three basic network-diagnostic commands were run on
  this machine.

  1. Create (if it does not already exist) the directory  
     /home/user/audit

  2. Inside that directory create a single plain-text file named  
     /home/user/audit/network_audit.log

  3. Capture the *full, unedited terminal output* of the following three
     commands **in the exact order shown** and place them inside the log
     file.  
        a) hostname -I  
        b) ip route  
        c) ping -c 2 127.0.0.1

  4. The log MUST follow this **strict framing format** so a downstream
     automated checker can validate it:

        ===== hostname -I START =====
        <verbatim output of “hostname -I”, exactly as the terminal prints it>
        ===== hostname -I END =====
        ===== ip route START =====
        <verbatim output of “ip route”>
        ===== ip route END =====
        ===== ping -c 2 127.0.0.1 START =====
        <verbatim output of “ping -c 2 127.0.0.1”>
        ===== ping -c 2 127.0.0.1 END =====

     • Every framing line must appear exactly as written above  
       (five equal signs, one space, the command string, one space,
       “START” or “END”, one space, five equal signs).  
     • There must be **one newline** after each framing line.  
     • There is no other text—only the six framing lines and the three blocks
       of raw command output.

  5. Do not wrap the command output, abbreviate it, or insert additional
     commentary.  The goal is a clean, deterministic audit artefact that
     another program can parse line-by-line.

  When finished, you can cat the file to verify it looks correct, but the
  final deliverable is simply the properly formatted
  /home/user/audit/network_audit.log file.
truth:
Expected artefact:
    Path: /home/user/audit/network_audit.log
    Required lines (exact literal strings that **must** be present):
      1. "===== hostname -I START ====="
      2. "===== hostname -I END ====="
      3. "===== ip route START ====="
      4. "===== ip route END ====="
      5. "===== ping -c 2 127.0.0.1 START ====="
      6. "===== ping -c 2 127.0.0.1 END ====="
    The order **must** be exactly as listed above.  
    Any bytes between a matching START/END pair are accepted as the raw
    command output and are **not** evaluated, so their exact content and
    length are flexible.

  Directory:
    /home/user/audit must exist and be user-writable.

  Permissions:
    network_audit.log must be at least readable (0644) so automated tests can
    open it.

  No other files are necessary for verification.
name: task_000000_e7961c27
